w:r.w:t: ⚠ This document is the single source of truth for the Jamisan ERP build. Every Claude Code session must open this file before touching any code. Do not modify this document during a build session.

Jamisan Global Nigeria Ltd. operates a COD (Cash on Delivery) ecommerce business currently managing 11,000+ orders per month across multiple product niches — primarily Gadgets and Beauty/Fashion. The current Google Sheets CRM breaks down past 15,000 row entries, requiring manual database splits that fragment historical analysis.

This CRM is a web-based, role-controlled, mobile-responsive application built to replace the Google Sheets system entirely. It must handle unlimited order volume, provide real-time dashboards, integrate with Pabbly, WATI, and Termii, and support future expansion across African countries.
COD — Cash on Delivery. No online payment gateway.
Google Sheets breaks at 15,000 rows — data split across multiple tabs
One unified database, unlimited scale, role-based access, real-time reporting
All 36 Nigerian states + FCT. Future: English-speaking African countries
HTML / CSS / HTMX + Alpine.js (mobile responsive). HTMX handles server-driven reactivity. Alpine.js handles interactive UI components. No build step required.
JWT-based with role permissions + 2FA for senior roles
Claude Code (CLI) — sessions anchored to this spec document
WATI — post-purchase sequences and agent notifications
Termii — CSR order alerts (managed in Pabbly, outside CRM)
Power BI — connected to PostgreSQL database
Every order follows this journey from creation to closure:
Prospecting or retargeting ad shown to customer
Customer visits pre-sales page and landing page
Customer fills Jamisan Form on the website
Order Bump and Upsell presented post-form
Webhook fires — order written to CRM database automatically
Order created with status: Interested. CSR assigned. Return customer flag set.
Email notification, WhatsApp follow up, SMS to CSR via Termii/Pabbly
CSR calls customer to confirm order. Status updated from dropdown.
CSR selects agent from state-filtered dropdown. Status auto-sets to Pending.
CSR clicks Copy button. Pastes formatted order into WhatsApp agent group.
Agent delivers to customer and collects cash (COD)
Status updated to Cash Paid or Cash Remitted
Cash Paid / Cash Remitted triggers post-purchase WATI sequence
Agent remits cash. Accountant logs remittance in Finance section.
Order closed. Stock decremented. Agent performance updated.

32 statuses exist. Each order holds one active status at a time — sequential override. Previous status is not stored on the order record but is captured in the audit log. Additional context is added by CSR in the Comments field.
⚠ Status logic: Pabbly sends 'Interested' as the default on every new order. CSR updates from there. When agent is assigned, status auto-changes to Pending.

⚠ Two separate tracking tables exist: order_status_history for time-based status queries (used by alert pills and Power BI), and audit_log for general system actions (edits, deletions, logins). Keeping them separate ensures dashboard queries stay fast.
CSR logs scheduled date in Sch Date field
Triggers WATI post-purchase sequence. DECREMENTS stock permanently.
Triggers WATI sequence. Does NOT decrement stock — already decremented at Cash Paid.
One attempt only. Returns to agent warehouse.
Warehouse Coordinator logs in Trashed Items
Row highlighted RED. Full payment required before next delivery.
Price field updated, reason in Comments
Auto-set after 3 days (72 hours) on Pending with no update
Only Ops Mgr and Directors can delete
⚠ No self-registration. Directors and HR Managers can create staff accounts. Directors can disable any account instantly. Disabled accounts cannot log in until re-enabled by Director.
Create/delete users, disable accounts, CSV export, delete orders, view all dashboards
Oversees CRM hygiene and staff accountability
Delete duplicate orders, view all CSR metrics, receive login alerts
Calls customers, confirms orders, updates statuses
Update status, add comments, assign agents, copy button
Agent remittance, financial reports, taxes, payroll
Log remittances, view financial data, payroll access
Every login and logout is logged per staff member with timestamp
Director sees who is currently active in the CRM at any time
Last login and last logout visible per staff per day
Monday to Saturday: if a CSR has not logged in by 9:00 AM, Director and Ops Manager receive an automatic email alert listing who has not logged in
CSR sees only orders in their assigned department
Pabbly assigns CSR and department on order creation — not manual
Products are department-locked. No cross-department orders.
Always within the same department as the main product
New departments can be added without restructuring permissions
Fixed per product. Editable by Director only.
Which brand or store this product belongs to
Three stock states: Available, Reserved, Decremented
Available = Waybill Stock minus Reserved minus Decremented
Reserved: order status changes to Pending (assigned to agent) — stock is soft-allocated, physically with agent
Decremented: order status changes to Cash Paid — stock permanently removed
Released back to Available: order status changes to Failed, Returned, Abandoned, or Cancelled — Reserved stock freed
Cash Remitted does NOT decrement stock — already decremented at Cash Paid
Abandoned after 3 days (72 hours) auto-trigger releases Reserved stock back to Available
Main Inventory and Order Bump Inventory tracked separately
Out of Stock status on an order does NOT affect stock calculation — it is a CSR flag only
Logged by Warehouse Coordinator when failed delivery returns from agent
Fields: Product Name, Colour, Quantity, From State, Agent, Status, Month, Year
Agent inspects returned item — good condition: redistribute to another customer
Bad condition: agent returns physical item, Warehouse Coordinator logs as Trashed
⚠ A separate 'customers' table is maintained for fast lookups. This avoids scanning 100k+ order rows on every page load for colour coding.
Primary unique identifier for every customer
Running count of all orders placed
Running count of Cash Paid / Cash Remitted orders only
new / ordered_not_bought / bought — drives row colour coding
Boolean — true if customer is currently banned
Total amount paid across all orders
customer_tag = ordered_not_bought — has ordered before but never completed a purchase
customer_tag = bought — has at least one Cash Paid or Cash Remitted order
is_banned = true — banned customer. Overrides all other colours.
customer_tag = new — first time customer, no history
Clicking on a customer name or phone number opens a full customer profile panel containing:
Personal details — name, phone, other phone, email, sex, full address history
Ban status — red banner if banned, reason and date visible
Full order history — every order ever placed, chronological, with status
Purchase history — what they bought, when, quantities, amounts paid
Complaint history — all complaints ever logged against this customer
CSR notes — freeform note field at customer level, separate from order comments
Lifetime value — total amount paid across all time
⚠ Return customer detection: When a new order arrives, CRM checks phone number against customers table. If match found, Return Customer field is set to Yes automatically — no Pabbly input required.
Individual contact at the agent company
Any delivery restrictions for this agent
Display name of the agent's WhatsApp group e.g. Shanoma Logistics Jamisan
Invite link to the agent's WhatsApp group — used by CSR Copy Waybill to route to correct group
CSR selects State on the order form
Agent dropdown automatically filters to show only agents covering that state
CSR selects agent — status automatically changes to Pending
CSR clicks Copy button — formatted order message is copied to clipboard
CSR pastes into the relevant WhatsApp agent group manually
CSR can override the auto-filtered agent by selecting any agent in the state from the dropdown
All overrides are logged in the audit trail
One-click copy generates the following formatted message:
Total Price to be paid by customer: [Price]
A zone_assignments table exists in the database from Day 1
Fields: state, lga, primary_agent, overflow_agent, dispatch_rule, zone_belt, is_active
V1: Used only to filter agent dropdown by state
V2: Intelligent auto-assignment based on LGA-to-agent mapping
States currently mapped: Lagos (partial), Ogun (complete V2), Enugu (complete V1), FCT/Abuja (complete V1), Delta (partial)
Target: All 36 states + FCT — 774 LGAs total
One attempt only — delivery is expensive
Returns to agent's own warehouse — no central warehouse
Each agent operates their own warehouse independently
Auto-set to Abandoned after 3 days (72 hours) on Pending with no update
Full payment required upfront before any delivery attempt
Agent Notifications — Abandoned and Pending Orders
Automated email to agent's registered email address
Automated WATI message to agent's phone number
Daily summary — both channels fire simultaneously
Same as Copy Button format — pre-formatted order details
To be defined on Day 3 — recommended: daily at 8am
Agent delivers order and collects cash from customer
Accountant logs remittance in Finance section
Fields: Date, Agent, State, Amount Remitted, Logistics Fee Paid by Us, Final Amount Remitted, Month, Year, Comment
Daily Financial Analysis shows per-agent: Sold Items, Logistics, Returns, Waybill Sending/Receiving, To Be Remitted, Remitted, Balance
Delivery fee paid to agents per order
Cost of goods purchased for resale
Email marketing, WhatsApp marketing, graphics, bots, etc.
Sum of all Cash Paid + Cash Remitted order prices
Total expenses ÷ total sales (Cash Paid + Cash Remitted)
Date Paid minus Date Ordered — how long customer took to pay
Today minus Date Ordered — how old the order is
On login, the CSR sees a tab bar above their order grid. Tabs replace alert pills — calmer, always visible, and tell the CSR exactly where work is waiting without feeling like an emergency.
Full department grid, newest first, 100 per page. Default tab on login.
Cash Paid orders where logistics_fee is null or zero. Red badge shows count if above zero.
Orders in Pending status for 4 or more days. Red badge shows count if above zero.
Orders with status = CSR Skipped. Red badge shows count if above zero.
Orders still sitting at Interested status — unworked orders. Red badge shows count if above zero.

Badge behaviour: Red circle with white font showing the count appears only when count is above zero. A clean day shows tabs with no badges. A problematic day shows red badges on relevant tabs. Clicking any tab filters the grid instantly via HTMX partial update — no page reload.
Ops Manager and Director see team-wide counts aggregated across all CSRs with per-staff breakdown in their own dashboard view.
CSR receives their own hygiene summary. Ops Manager and Director CC'd.
Outstanding items per CSR — missing fees, unpaid cash orders, stale statuses
If CSR has not logged in by 9:00 AM Mon-Sat, Director and Ops Manager receive alert
Abandoned and Pending orders assigned to agent
Automated via WATI to agent phone
Primary webhook input — Jamisan Form submissions auto-populate CRM
CRM must accept webhooks from any provider (Zapier, Make etc.) — not Pabbly-locked
Webhook receives: Order ID, Date, Source, CSR, Name, Phone, Address, Product, Price, Qty, Order Bump details
On receipt: order is created with status Interested, return customer check runs automatically
Cash Paid status → triggers post-purchase buyer sequence
Cash Remitted status → triggers post-purchase buyer sequence
Complained / refund customers → removed from post-purchase sequence
Agent notifications → daily Pending/Abandoned order messages
V1: WATI triggered manually via Cash Paid Orders export (last 300)
V2: Direct WATI API integration built into CRM
SMS alerts to CSR when a new order arrives
Currently managed entirely in Pabbly — outside the CRM
No CRM integration needed in V1
PostgreSQL database connected directly to Power BI
No API needed — Power BI reads from database directly
CRM does not need to push data to Power BI
Every new order must have a unique Order ID auto-generated by the CRM
Historical orders without Order IDs to be backfilled before data migration
Manual orders entered through CRM get auto-generated Order IDs — no manual input
A separate counter table tracks the last Order ID to prevent duplicates
Director Dashboard — Based on Power BI Report Structure
Year, Quarter, Month, Week, Day, Niche
Orders → Dropped Off → Abandoned → Sales funnel
Conversion Rate — overall and by gender
Total Orders and Conversion Rate by State
Total Orders and Conversion Rate by CSR
Total Orders and Conversion Rate by Product
Conversion Rate by Day of Week
Cost of Order and Cost of Sale
Expense breakdown — Logistics, Procurement, Advertising, Shipping
CSR performance metrics — conversion rate, orders, sales per CSR
Percentage of complaints and returns per CSR
Login tracking — who is active, who has not logged in today
Alert pill summary — outstanding CRM hygiene issues per CSR
Directors, Ops Manager, Accountant — 30-day trusted device period
Minimum 8 characters, must include number and special character
Director can disable any account instantly — login blocked until re-enabled
Director disables account in CRM. Company email deactivated separately.
Ops Manager and Directors must type DELETE in capitals to confirm any deletion
Every status change, edit, and deletion is logged — who, what changed, when
Directors only — full database export. No CSV access for any other role.
Every login, logout, and session duration logged per staff member
No CSV download, no select-all, no bulk export for CSRs. Phone numbers visible but API endpoints are rate-limited to prevent automated scraping. All profile views logged in audit trail.
Dedicated indexed table for status change history — separate from general audit_log for fast Power BI and alert pill queries
⚠ Full UI/UX design to be completed on Day 3 — User Flows. Rules below are locked from Day 1 decisions.
Orange = ordered not bought. Green = bought. Red = banned. White = new.
Inline edit or edit panel — on every order row
Requires type-DELETE confirmation. Ops Manager and Directors only.
One-click copy of pre-formatted WhatsApp message per order row
Prominent pills at top of CSR dashboard on login. Clickable — filters to relevant orders.
Click customer name/phone → full history panel opens
All views must work on mobile. Agent portal is mobile-first (V2).
Login page only. No signup link.
Director UI shows active/disabled toggle per staff member
CSR default view shows only their department orders
⚠ V1 is Nigeria-only. Schema must support multi-country from Day 1 to avoid costly migrations later.
Equivalent of Nigerian States — labelled dynamically per country (State / Region / Province)
Equivalent of Nigerian LGAs — labelled dynamically per country (LGA / District / Division)
When CSR selects country, form labels update automatically. Nigeria shows State/LGA. Ghana shows Region/District.
Country-code aware. Nigeria = +234. Ghana = +233. Auto-formatted on input.
currency_code field per order. Not hardcoded to Naira (NGN).
Timezone stored per order based on country. Not hardcoded to Nigerian time.
English only in V1. Structure supports localisation in V2.
Each module is built in a separate Git branch. Main branch is always stable. No module touches another module's files.
All 52 tables, relationships, indexes, seed data
Pabbly webhook, Order ID generation, return customer check
Order database view, status updates, copy button, alert pills
User management, delete, CSV export, audit log view
Remittance logging, expense tracking, agent ledger, financial dashboard
Director and Ops dashboards, all charts and KPIs, CSR weekly report
Cash Paid triggers, agent notifications, sequence management
Mobile responsive, performance, VPS deployment, Power BI connection
⚠ Each Claude Code session begins by reading this SPEC.md file. The session then reads the current module's branch files. No session proceeds without both.
18. Additional Features — Added During Spec Week
The following features were identified during Day 3 review and added to the schema. They are fully specced and must be built into the relevant modules.
Table: stock_adjustments. Handles warehouse reality — missing, stolen, damaged, or surplus items discovered during stocktakes.
Phantom stock — system shows items as available but they are physically missing. CSRs assign orders that cannot be fulfilled.
If adjustment pushes available below zero — warn and require Director approval
AFTER INSERT: UPDATE inventory SET waybill_stock = waybill_stock + NEW.quantity_adjusted
130+ negative quantity waybill entries import as Initial_Count_Correction records
Director dashboard shows monthly NGN value of all stock losses
Table: agent_ledger. Chronological bank statement per agent. Auto-populated by system triggers — no manual entry. Resolves balance disputes instantly.
Agent balance disputes — currently takes Accountant hours to reconstruct manually from multiple sheets
Cash Paid → debit. Logistics fee logged → credit. Waybill (agent paid) → credit. Remittance logged → credit.
Calculated by PostgreSQL trigger on every INSERT
Daily summary with expandable rows per day
Today minus last remittance date. Zero balance = Settled.
Grand total + full agent table with colour coding. Red = 7+ days, Yellow = 3-6, Green = 0-2, Grey = Settled
PDF/CSV statement per agent for dispute resolution
Table: department_targets. Replaces hardcoded 70% planned sales rate with configurable targets per department. Directors only can update.
Minimum acceptable rate per department. Flags underperformance.
Used for Planned Sales on CSR weekly report. Was hardcoded 70%.
Historical reports use rate active at that time
Planned Sales = total orders x planned_sales_rate per department

Replaces the current manual process: extract figures from Google Sheets → type into MS Word → convert to PDF → email. In the ERP this is one-click PDF generation from live data. Estimated time saving: 2-3 hours per week across the team.
Conversion rate, total orders, total sales, sales gap vs target. Source: orders + department_targets.
Per CSR: orders, sales, conversion rate, benchmark status, planned sales. Source: orders + csr_performance_monthly + department_targets.

Per product per CSR: all status columns. CSR Skipped and Interested flagged red if > 0 at period end. Failure reasons and complaints pulled automatically. Also used as the CSR Personal Report (Tab 3 in CSR Reports screen).

Running balance per product. Dispatches, returns, waybill movements, trashed items, stock adjustments within the period. Source: inventory + goods_movement + waybill_expenses + trashed_items + stock_adjustments.
Complaints grouped by product, type, resolution, cost. Source: complaints + orders + failure_reason_codes.
Per agent: orders, sales, pending, abandoned, balance, days outstanding. Includes current running_balance and days outstanding from agent_ledger.
Revenue, expenses breakdown (ads, procurement, shipping, logistics), profit/loss, net margin %, CPO, CPS. Source: orders + all expense tables.
Amount to be remitted, amount remitted, outstanding balance per agent. Source: agent_remittance + agent_ledger.
Manual entry by Accountant. Providus Inflow/Outflow/Comments, Moniepoint Inflow/Outflow/Comments, Accountant Notes. Generate button blocked until Accountant saves this section.
Manual free text entered by Ops Manager before PDF generation.
CSR Skipped and Interested highlighted red if above zero at end of reporting period. Zero is the expected value.
Generate Full Report button only activates when cash_flow_logged = true (Accountant has saved Section 9).
Jamisan-branded. All 10 sections. Stored to Google Drive. Link saved in weekly_reports table for historical reference.

Tracks all third-party suppliers and service providers that support operations. Separate from agents who handle last-mile delivery. Vendors supply goods, materials, or logistics services to Jamisan directly.
Shipping Agent, Warehouse Agent, Packaging Supplier, Print Supplier, Other — free text to allow new categories over time
name, phone_1, phone_2, address, city, state (admin_level_1), LGA (admin_level_2), country
Freeform description of the service this vendor provides
Ops Manager and Directors can add and edit vendors
Agents handle last-mile delivery. Vendors supply goods and services to Jamisan directly.
Shipping Agent (international freight), Warehouse Agent (storage), Packaging Supplier (packing sacks), Print Supplier (printed courier bags)
19. CSR Interface — Final UI/UX Specification (v3 — Wireframe Locked)

Design foundation: Airtable-style grid. This spec is the design system for all other modules. Every other role inherits the sidebar, colour palette, grid pattern, tab pattern, and panel pattern from this module.
⚠ Design inspiration: Airtable CRM grid (see wireframe slide 2). Mockups built in Adobe Fireworks and Photoshop by Director. Purple annotations in wireframe = instructions only, not design elements.
Full black background. Centered dark card with subtle border. Jamisan logo with orange A mark at top.
Email address or username (accepts both — e.g. foluke.a or email). Password with lock icon.
Opens modal: 'If you have forgotten your password, username or email address, kindly reach out to the operations manager.' Orange header bar, X close button.
Director creates all accounts. No registration shown.

Rich black. Jamisan logo + white wordmark in header. Red circular toggle with white chevron pointing LEFT when expanded. Active item = white text + icon. Inactive = muted orange/grey. Settings gear bottom anchored.
Icons only strip. Red circular toggle with white chevron pointing RIGHT. Maximises grid space.
Fixed footer inside sidebar. Shows employee avatar (photo if uploaded, else initial), First Name + Last Initial (e.g. Faith A.), Logout icon to right.
Photo if uploaded by employee. Initial circle if not.
Top right. Green = success. Red = error. Auto-dismisses 3 seconds.
Orange underline bar appears beneath active item. Clicking any item switches the workspace without page reload.
Primary master grid. Default view on login. Where CSRs spend 90% of their time.
Dedicated workspace for Scheduled and Committed Scheduled orders only.
Internal agent directory — hub addresses, phone numbers, WhatsApp group links. CSRs look up agents here, especially new staff.
CSR personal metrics and team leaderboard.
Tab bar sits above the grid. Active tab = white background. Inactive = muted orange. Red circular badge shows count if above zero. Clicking filters grid instantly — no page reload.
All orders newest to oldest. No time restriction. No badge count.
Orders still at Interested status. Excludes last 24 hours (fresh orders). Shows 25 hours to 1 week ago only. If CSR has done their job, Interested should clear within 24 hours.
Orders with blank Comments field. Now to 1 week ago.
Orders with Pending status. Now to 1 week ago.
Cash Paid orders with logistics_fee = 0 or null. Up to 3 months ago.
Cash Paid orders with date_paid empty. No time limit.
Orders with Abandoned status. Now to 1 week ago.
Orders tagged Not Picking Calls, Unreachable, Call Back. Now to 72 hours ago.
Any Pending order sitting for exactly 72 hours (3 days) is automatically switched to Abandoned by a cron job. No human input required.
Popup explaining the traffic light colour coding system.
Focus mode toggle. When active: button becomes blue pill with white X to exit. Icon becomes open eye, text turns white. Filters grid to logged-in CSR's orders only.

Airtable-style multi-condition master filter panel. Structure: Where / And / Or + [Field dropdown] + [Operator dropdown: contains, is, etc.] + [Value input] + [Delete row icon]. Add condition and Add condition group at bottom. Copy from another view option.
Rapid filter dropdown to show only Green, Red, or Orange rows.
Expandable text field for phone number or Order ID search.
REMOVED — column header funnel + master filter + Color button covers all sorting needs.
Top right. Jamisan Gold button. Opens blank slide-out panel to manually create new order (Workflow B).
Column headers are sticky — remain visible during vertical scroll.
Every column header has a funnel icon [▽] for instant column-level filtering — same pattern as Google Sheets.
Enabled — all 17 columns visible by scrolling right.
Default 150 rows per page. Adjustable up to 1000. Navigation: first, previous, page numbers, next, last.
ordered_at DESC — newest first. Lead decay is real in COD.
Primary key. Locked. Clicking opens slide-out detail panel.
Locked for CSRs. Editable ONLY by Ops Manager/Director via dropdown.
Days From Order. Locked. Auto-calculates: today minus creation date.
Editable inline. Clickable — WhatsApp on desktop, call on mobile.
Editable inline — 30-char truncated preview. Full text in slide-out panel.
Editable inline. Blank until Cash Paid.
On every new order, system checks Phone Number, Other Number, and Email against entire historical database.
Match found AND any previous order has status = Banned. New row turns deep red. New order status forced to Banned automatically.
Match found AND previous orders exist BUT none of them are Cash Paid. Customer has ordered before but never paid.
Match found AND at least one previous order has status = Cash Paid. Row turns bright green with white text.
No match found. Brand new customer. No history.
Triggered by clicking Order ID or New button. Covers approximately 75% of screen from right side. Grid remains visible on left.
Navigate to previous/next order without closing panel. Hidden if at top or bottom of list.
Manual save button. System also auto-saves when user clicks out of a field. Shows 'Saving...' during auto-save.
Copies Name, Phone, Address, Product, Price to clipboard in WhatsApp-ready format for agent group chat.
Red status text. Changes to Saving... during auto-save.
< collapses right intelligence panel. > expands it. Allows full-width left form when needed.
Read-only header: Order #10000 for Ohambele (FJAMG1271) - DFO (3 Days). Sub-header: Order ID - Customer Name (time | date).
19.10 Detail Panel — Workflow A (Editing Existing Order)
Designed for live follow-up calls. Fields ordered to match the natural conversation flow.
Phone Number, Other Number, Status dropdown, Comments (large text area).
Agent dropdown + chain link icon beside it. Clicking link icon opens state's Agent Distribution Strategy file in new tab.
Main Product, Quantity, Order Bump dropdown (None by default — selecting product reveals Bump Product and Bump Qty). Logistics Fee.
19.11 Detail Panel — Workflow B (Creating New Order)
Triggered by New button. Designed for raw data entry from a WhatsApp chat screenshot.
Phone Number (entering triggers DB lookup — auto-fills Name and previous State if customer found), Other Number, Name.
Main Product, Quantity, Product Price, Order Bump mechanics.
Agent dropdown + strategy link icon, Logistics Fee.
Total Orders (lifetime), Cash Paid Orders (lifetime), Delivery Success Rate (Cash Paid ÷ Total Orders %), Total Lifetime Value (₦ sum of all Cash Paid orders).
Dynamic coloured pills e.g. Verified Buyer (green dot). Driven by customer_tag field.
Vertically scrollable timeline of previous order cards — product bought/rejected and CSR comments per order.
Read-only developer verification section. Shows: Assigned Weekday, System Log Time, Last Modified Timestamp, API Round-Robin Batch ID. Not for CSR use — confirms calculations are running correctly.
Right panel can be collapsed to give left form full width. Expand icon in top bar restores it.
19.13 Gap Fixes — UI Elements Added After Full Spec Review
⚠ These four UI elements were identified as gaps during cross-referencing of the Day 1/2 spec against the UI/UX sprint. All are now locked.
The combined weekly report replaces a 2-3 hour manual process. A dedicated screen is needed for manual text input before PDF generation.

Tab 3 in the CSR Reports screen. Step 1: date range (defaults to last Friday to this Thursday). Step 2: Any Other Business text area. Step 3: Generate My Report button. Downloads Section 3 PDF — product breakdown, unresponsive list, failed/returns auto-pulled. CSR sends manually via email.

Dedicated screen in Ops Manager Reports section. Shows all 10 sections in preview. Manual text areas: Any Other Business (Ops Manager fills). Accountant Notes section shows as pending until Accountant fills it. Generate Full Report button only activates when Accountant Notes is filled.

Section 9 of the Ops Full Report. Sub-section in Accountant Finance module. Fields: Providus Inflow (₦), Providus Outflow (₦), Providus Comments, Moniepoint Inflow (₦), Moniepoint Outflow (₦), Moniepoint Comments, Accountant Notes text area (mandatory). Clicking Save sets cash_flow_logged = true — clears Pending Actions reminder and enables the Generate button for Ops Manager.
Jamisan-branded. Accountability columns (CSR Skipped, Interested) flagged red if above zero. Scheduled column combines Committed Scheduled and Committed Immediate.
Gap 2 — Lessons Learned in CSR Sidebar and Director Approval Queue

🧠 Lessons Learned added to CSR left sidebar between Inventory and Complaints. Read-only for CSRs — shows Published lessons only. Searchable by category and keyword. Any staff can click Submit a Lesson to propose a new entry.
Title, category dropdown (from lesson_categories), content text area. Submits with status = Pending. CSR sees confirmation: Your submission is pending Director review.

In Director Approvals and Administration panel — Pending Submissions section. Shows: title, category, submitted by, submission date. Director clicks Review — slide-out opens with full content. Two buttons: Publish as Official (status → Published) and Reject with Reason (mandatory text field). Rejected submissions notify the submitter.
Visible to all staff in their Lessons Learned sidebar item. Searchable, filterable by category.
Vendors directory added as a sidebar item alongside Agents. Shows: Name, Type, Phone 1, Phone 2, State, Comments. Ops Manager can add and edit vendor profiles.

Vendor field in the procurement expense entry form is a dropdown linked to the vendors table — not a free-text field. Chain link icon beside the dropdown opens the vendor's full profile in a slide-out for reference.
Shipping Agent, Warehouse Agent, Packaging Supplier, Print Supplier, Other — from vendor_type field.
Director Approvals and Administration panel — Settings sub-section.

Base Conversion Rate % (minimum acceptable), Target Conversion Rate % (strong performer benchmark), Planned Sales Rate % (used for Planned Sales calculation on CSR report — was hardcoded 70% in Google Sheets).
Date picker. Changes take effect from this date. Historical reports use the rate active at report time — never retroactively changed.
Shows current active values alongside input fields so Director can see what they are changing before saving.
Logs change in audit_log with Director user_id, old values, new values, effective_from date. Confirmation toast: Department targets updated successfully.

Centralises tribal knowledge under strict executive oversight. All staff can submit and read published lessons. Only Directors can publish or reject. Prevents knowledge living in WhatsApp chats and individual heads.
Searchable database of approved sales scripts, logistics warnings, CRM SOPs, and fraud prevention tactics
Directors only — no peer upvotes, no staff approval
Staff submits → status = Pending → Director reviews → Publish as Official or Reject with mandatory reason
Managed via lesson_categories lookup table. Directors add new categories. Starting set: Sales Script, Logistics Warning, CRM SOP, Fraud Prevention, Other
Fixed dropdown from lookup table — prevents freeform drift. Director-managed over time.
Full-text search on title, content, and category for all Published lessons
Secure access control, payroll data preparation, and digital punch-clock reporting.
All employees with role assignments. HR and Directors can assign or revoke RBAC roles.
Director creates account → username auto-generated (firstname.lastinitial) → temporary password sent to employee email
Director sets is_active = false → immediate lockout → all active sessions invalidated instantly → audit log entry created
Visible to HR, Directors, and Ops Manager only. Not visible to the employee themselves.
Login timestamps (session_log.login_at), logout timestamps (session_log.logout_at), idle time (session_log.last_active_at updated on every API request), daily order volume, daily audit activity count
last_active_at field added to session_log. Application updates on every user API request. Idle = gap between last_active_at and next action.
HR pulls payroll records, commission calculations, and bonus logs for Director approval before payment
users, roles, employee_profiles, session_log (+ last_active_at), payroll, audit_log, weekly_bonus_log

The Ops Manager is the air traffic controller of the operation. UI philosophy: high-alert, exception-based management. The system aggressively flags what is broken so the Ops Manager does not have to search for it.
20.1 Global Shell — Inherits CSR Design System
Same rich black sidebar, same toggle behaviour, same toast notifications, same global shell as CSR module. RBAC unlocks a different suite of tools.
Command Center (default), Master Orders, Agent Remittances, Staff Roster, Audit and Exceptions

Bottom left of sidebar next to CSR name and avatar. Coffee cup icon ☕. When clicked: entire bottom bar turns amber, text changes to On Break — click to resume. Applies to CSR role only — not Ops Manager.
Any CRM action within last 20 minutes. Receives leads from Pabbly assignment.
No CRM action for 20 to 45 minutes. Shows on Ops Manager Live Floor.
CSR clicks On Break toggle. Status flips immediately without waiting for timer.
Logged out or no CRM action for more than 45 minutes.
last_active_at field on session_log table. Updated on every API request from the user.
Pabbly assigns CSRs at ingestion as normal — no status check at Pabbly level. If a CSR is Away and receives leads, Ops Manager bulk reassigns manually using the Live Floor panel.
Staff Roster shows exact timestamp when CSR went Away — Ops Manager can distinguish a 15-minute lunch from a 2-hour disappearance.
F-shape reading pattern. Most critical business risks at the top. Filters: Today/date range, All Departments.
Three alert cards at top: Floating Cash (₦ total across agents), Stale Deliveries (orders with no status update for 96+ hours), CSR Idle Alerts (count of offline CSRs during shift).

Left panel. Agent name, unremitted ₦, oldest debt in days. Clicking any agent routes directly to Agent Remittances sidebar module filtered to that agent. Clicking View Full Agent Ledger opens full agent remittances view.
Right panel. CSR Name, Live Status (🟢/🟡/🔴), Leads Handled today, Conversion Rate, Away timestamp if applicable. Reassign Leads button.
Fire alarm — shows immediate threats only. Full detail lives in sidebar modules.
Orders escalated via CSR Request Unban button. Shows CSR's mandatory explanation. Ops Manager clicks Unban User to release order.
Orders where CSR attempted Cash Paid with ₦0 logistics fee. Ops Manager reads comment and clicks Approve.
General escalation raised by CSR with mandatory reason code: Fraud Suspected, Agent Refusing Pickup, Wrong Item Shipped, or other approved codes from escalation_reason_codes table.
Every escalation requires Ops Manager action — Approve, Reject, or Reassign. Cannot be left open.
Same 17-column grid as CSR but with additional Override Status column.
Editable dropdown for Ops Manager and Directors. Locked for CSRs.
Force Status, Unban User, Approve Zero Fee — role-restricted actions.
Select multiple rows, use bulk-action dropdown to change CSR. Used when a CSR goes Away with unworked leads.
Top right. CSV export — Directors only.
All, Escalations (count badge), Banned Overrides (count badge), Zero-Fee Approvals (count badge).
Editable dropdown — Ops Manager can reassign order to any CSR.
Ops Manager has additional Audit Trail tab in right panel alongside Customer History. Shows forensic log: timestamp, field changed, old value, new value, user who changed it.
Unban User button and Zero Fee Approve button visible in panel if order is in escalation state.
Full ledger of all agents with unremitted cash. Daily summary with expandable rows per agent.
Clicks Clear Debt — creates new row in agent_remittance with status = Pending_Bank_Verification. Removes red alert from dashboard.
Logs in, matches against actual bank statement, clicks Verified and Cleared to finalise.
Person managing agents cannot be same person balancing company bank account. Ops logs, Accountant verifies.
Red = 7+ days outstanding, Yellow = 3-6 days, Green = 0-2 days, Grey = Settled.
Live performance and capacity matrix. Dense read-only grid.
CSR Name and Live Status (🟢/🟡/🔴) with Away timestamp if applicable.
Shift Start Time — first login of the day from session_log.
Fresh Leads Assigned Today — orders created today assigned to this CSR.
Leads Touched — orders where first_contact_at IS NOT NULL, meaning CSR actively called or worked the lead.
Current Conversion Rate — today's Cash Paid divided by today's total orders.
Hygiene Counts — count of this CSR's orders in each hygiene tab (Missing Logistics, Pending, CSR Skipped, No Date Paid).
Last Activity — exact timestamp of last CRM action from last_active_at.
Uche can instantly see if a CSR has capacity for more leads or is bottlenecked, and act without a WhatsApp message.
Warehouse Coordinator submits shrinkage log → surfaces in Audit and Exceptions feed. Ops Manager investigates, clicks Authorize Write-off to deduct from database and notify Accountant.
Searchable by user, action, date range, order ID. Full forensic trail.
All rule-breaking events — unauthorized price changes, duplicate orders, Pabbly reconciliation discrepancies — surface here.
Lookup table. Director-managed. Starting values: Banned_Customer_Unban_Request, Fraud_Suspected, Agent_Refusing_Pickup, Wrong_Item_Shipped, Zero_Fee_Approval_Request, Other.
Links order_id, reason_code_id, raised_by (CSR user_id), raised_at, resolved_by (Ops Manager user_id), resolved_at, resolution_action, resolution_notes.
Existing table gains status field: Pending_Bank_Verification, Verified_Cleared. Also gains logged_by for Ops Manager user_id.

The Director dashboard replaces Power BI entirely. UI philosophy: Interactive God-View. High-contrast data visualisation. Every number is a live query from the PostgreSQL database. Two Directors — dashboard always shows full company view, never personalised.
All modules unlocked — CSR Workspace, Ops Command Center, Master Orders, Agent Remittances, Staff Roster, Audit and Exceptions, HR, Finance, Inventory, Complaints, Lessons Learned, Settings
Required on login. 30-day trusted device period.
Top right. WYSIWYG PDF export of current filtered view. Also offers Download Raw Data CSV for Accountant to use in QuickBooks or Xero.
Sticky top header. Changing any filter reloads all charts cleanly from the server. One source of truth. No cross-filtering in V1 — global filters only.
Today, This Week, This Month, Q1, YTD, Custom range
Changing any filter triggers full server re-fetch. All charts reload with clean data. No partial state.
Cross-filtering — clicking a bar in one chart filters all other charts. Deferred because multi-variable state management across independent charts is too risky in V1 and can corrupt data silently.
Sits directly below the filter bar. First thing the Director sees on login.
SUM of orders.price WHERE status = Cash Paid. Count of sales shown beneath.
SUM across ad_expenses + procurement_expenses + waybill_expenses + expenses tables for the period.
Gross Revenue minus Total Expenses. Colour coded — green positive, red negative.
Four sub-cards: Ads (ad_expenses), Procurement (procurement_expenses), Shipping (waybill_expenses), Logistics (logistics fees from orders).
Total Expenses ÷ Total Orders. Computed at query time, not stored.
Total Expenses ÷ Total Cash Paid Orders. Computed at query time. More meaningful than CPO in COD because many orders never convert.
Cash Paid ÷ Total Orders × 100. Shown beneath CPS for context.
Cash Paid only. Cash Remitted excluded. Matches spec decision from Day 1.
Lower half of dashboard. Charts are independent static readouts in V1. Global filter bar drives all filtering. Chart types confirmed from Director's existing P&L Excel sheet.
Tab A — Sales Funnel and Demographics

Master Funnel waterfall: Raw Orders → Dropped Off → Abandoned → Cash Paid Sales → Final Conversion Rate. Conversion by Sex donut: Male, Female, Undefined (Undefined shown as third segment — not hidden). Conversion Rate by Day of Week horizontal bar chart.

CSR Leaderboard combo chart: Valid Orders (bar) vs Conversion Rate (line). Agent Delivery Rate combo chart: Total Assigned (bar) vs Success Rate (line). Links to Full Staff Audit Log and Export Agent Performance PDF.

Orders and Conversion by State horizontal bar. Product Winners combo chart. Revenue per product per month table — matches P&L product-level row structure. Order Source breakdown — which landing page or channel is driving orders.
Monthly trend line showing Net Profit/Loss trajectory. Matches NET PROFIT/LOSS chart in Director's Excel P&L. Critical for seeing trajectory at a glance.
Total Sales (blue line) vs Total Expenses (red line) per month. Matches TOTAL SALES and TOTAL EXPENSES chart in Director's Excel. Shows revenue-expense gap trend.

Percentage of each expense category. Matches Director's existing Excel donut chart. Categories: Procurement, Advertising, Salary, Shipping, Subscriptions, Stationery, Media, Equipment, WayBill, and all other expense_categories.
Net P/L line chart and Sales vs Expenses combo sit in the Financial Hero section below the KPI cards. Expense donut sits alongside. Three analytics tabs below.
Dedicated section for Director-only operational duties. Surfaces items that require Director action. Separate from the analytics engine.
Adjustments that would push available stock below zero require Director sign-off before trigger fires.
Pending submissions awaiting Director review. Publish as Official or Reject with mandatory reason.
Set and update base_conversion_rate, target_conversion_rate, planned_sales_rate per department. Changes logged in audit trail with effective_from date.
Directors and HR Managers can create, disable, and manage user accounts.
Daily CRM vs Pabbly order count discrepancies surface here for Director review.
pg_dump success/failure status. Failed backup triggers email alert and appears here.
Director manages this lookup table — add new escalation categories as business evolves.
Director manages this lookup table — add new knowledge base categories.
Exports exactly what is currently visible on screen — current filter state, hero numbers, funnel, tab data. Jamisan-branded.
Exports the sanitised rows used to generate the current charts. Accountant can plug directly into QuickBooks or Xero.
Export reflects active filters. If filtered to Beauty + This Month, export contains only that data.
Full access to God-View grid — same as Ops Manager plus all override capabilities.
Can edit orders directly via slide-out panel. Rarely used but always available.
Every Director edit logged in audit_log with timestamp and previous value.

The Accountant's world is almost entirely within the Finance module. UI philosophy: ledger-style density. Double-entry verification and chronological financial logging. Landing page is always Pending Actions — task-first, not chart-first.
Pending Actions (default), Core Financials, Remittance Clearing, Marketing and Ads, Procurement and Waybills, Payroll and Admin
Pending Actions dashboard — shows outstanding items requiring Accountant action today
Read-only. Can view all orders. Cannot edit any field.
Required on login — Accountant handles financial data
Landing screen on every login. Each card shows a count. Clicking navigates to the relevant module. Clean day = all zeros.
COUNT(agent_remittance WHERE status = Pending_Bank_Verification). Red if 3+, Orange if 1-2, Green if 0.
COUNT of staff whose payroll for the current period is calculated but payment_date is null.
Configurable reminder — prompts to log weekly ad spend or recurring expenses if not yet entered.
Reminder to enter Providus and Moniepoint bank inflows/outflows for weekly report.
Two-step verification. Ops Manager logs cash extracted from agent. Accountant verifies against actual bank statement.
Log Date, Agent Name, ₦ Expected, Logged By (Ops Manager), Bank Ref / Ops Note, Verify Match button
Clicking opens slide-out panel. System asks for actual deposited amount. Accountant enters real figure. System auto-calculates variance (actual minus expected).
If variance exists: Accountant selects reason from remittance_variance_reason_codes (Agent Shortage, Bank Charges, Partial Payment, Transfer Error, Overpayment). Clicks Confirm Clearance.

Actual amount hits Gross Revenue only after Accountant clicks Verified and Cleared — not on Ops Manager logging. Agent_ledger Cash_Remitted credit fires on UPDATE when status = Verified_Cleared. Shortage auto-routes to Financial Losses expense category. Books balance automatically.
Variance triggers notification to Ops Manager and Director: agent name, expected amount, actual amount, shortage amount, reason code.
🟢 Fully Reconciled = zero variance. 🟡 Reconciled with Shortage = negative variance. 🔵 Reconciled with Overpayment = positive variance.
Read-only table below pending. Shows Clear Date, Agent, ₦ Cleared, ₦ Variance, Cleared By, Status.
Accountant adds bank transfer reference number on verification for audit trail.
Log Facebook and TikTok ad spend so Director CPO and CPS calculations are accurate and live.
Date, Platform, Campaign Name, Product, Amount Spent, Payment Method, Logged By, Receipt Link
Google Drive link — Accountant uploads screenshot from Ads Manager to Google Drive, pastes shareable link into receipt_url field. Clicking opens receipt in new tab.
Zone 1: Category + Date. Zone 2: Amount + Payment Method (Company Card, Bank Transfer, Petty Cash). Zone 3: Product + Platform + Campaign Name. Zone 4: Description + Receipt URL.
The moment Accountant saves an ad expense, Director dashboard recalculates CPO and CPS automatically. No manual spreadsheet required.
Log Alibaba and supplier purchases. USD and NGN stored. Exchange rate tracked per purchase for accurate FX history. Receipt URL field for invoice link.
Accountant or Ops Manager — both have access.
Log cost of every inter-state dispatch. Columns: Date, Origin, Destination Agent, Cargo, Transport Cost, Payment Method, Status.
Company Paid (HQ pays courier), Agent to Pay and Deduct (agent pays on delivery and deducts from remittance), Petty Cash.
Selecting this triggers automatic credit entry in agent_ledger (Waybill_Paid_By_Agent transaction type). Agent balance reduced automatically — no manual adjustment needed, no WhatsApp disputes.
Google Drive link to transport receipt or invoice.
Auto-calculates base salary + commission from csr_performance_monthly per staff per period. Weekly bonuses from weekly_bonus_log included.
Payroll batch cannot be marked as paid without Director approval. Accountant prepares, Director authorises, Accountant executes.
After external bank transfer: logs payment_date, payment_method, bank reference, currency_code.
Office costs, software subscriptions (WATI, Pabbly), miscellaneous. All with receipt URL field.
Log tax payments and obligations with receipt URL.
Google Drive link — no file upload to VPS. Accountant uploads to their Google Drive, copies shareable link, pastes into receipt_url field.
Simple URL field. Accountant pastes link. Clicking opens in new tab.
Direct upload to Google Drive via Drive API — skips manual copy-paste step.
UI philosophy: secure, auditable, list-based. Everything is logged. Sensitive PII is restricted strictly to HR and Directors. Punch-clock data is completely invisible to the employees being tracked.
Staff Directory (default), Digital Punch-Clock, Payroll Runs, Audit Logs
Staff Directory — the primary access control screen
HR Manager and Directors only. CSRs and other staff cannot access any HR screen.
Control panel for system access. HR and Directors can assign or revoke RBAC roles, onboard new staff, and instantly disable terminated employees.
Status (🟢/🔴/⚫), Name, Username, Role, Department, Last Login, Action (Edit button)
🟢 Active and logged in today. 🔴 Account disabled. ⚫ Never logged in.
Search by employee name. Filter by role dropdown.

Toggle System Access: Active → Disabled. The millisecond the switch flips: is_active = false written to database, active session token invalidated instantly, employee forced to login screen mid-action. audit_log entry stamped with exact timestamp. Cannot be undone without Director confirmation.
Top right. Opens onboarding slide-out panel.
HR types First Name and Last Name. System generates username as firstname.lastinitial (e.g. jane.d). Shown to HR, cannot be manually overridden.
System auto-generates and emails to employee. HR never sees the password.
Dropdown — all 9 roles. Determines RBAC permissions instantly on save.
Beauty or Gadgets — only relevant for CSR role.
Automatically set to L1 on creation. Cannot be manually changed by HR. Promoted automatically when csr_promotion_thresholds are crossed.
Full Name, Date of Birth, Gender, Phone, Email, Address, State, LGA
Job Title, Department, Employment Type, Start Date, Salary Grade
Next of Kin: name, relationship, phone, address. Emergency Contact: name, relationship, phone 1, phone 2, address.
NIN (National ID), Medical Conditions, Main Account Number, Alternate Account Number. All four encrypted at rest — even database administrator cannot read raw values.
Main Bank Name, Main Account Type (Savings/Current), Alternate Bank Name — accompany the encrypted account numbers.
Completely invisible to CSRs. Visible to HR, Directors, and Ops Manager only. Tracks productivity by date range and department.
Date (defaults to today), Department (all or specific)
CSV export for payroll processing or dispute resolution
Employee name with current live status (🟢/🟡/🔴)
First session_log.login_at for the selected date
Most recent session_log.logout_at. Shows Active if still logged in.
Sum of all session durations for the day
Calculated from gaps in last_active_at updates. Flagged with ⚠️ if above threshold.
COUNT of orders assigned to this CSR created on this date
COUNT of orders WHERE first_contact_at IS NOT NULL — CSR actively called or worked the lead
Cash Paid ÷ total orders for the day
Count of this CSR's orders sitting in Missing Logistics, Pending, CSR Skipped, No Date Paid tabs
COUNT of audit_log entries for this user on this date — every save, status change, panel open
Exact timestamp of last CRM action from last_active_at
last_active_at updated on every API request. Gap calculation: now() minus last_active_at. Flags shown for gaps above 20 minutes during active session.
session_log (login/logout/last_active_at), orders (leads assigned/touched), audit_log (action count). No new storage needed.
Employee Name + Level (e.g. Faith A. L2), Base Salary, Paid Units, Conversion %, Commission Tier, Total Commission, Weekly Bonuses, Total Pay

Base Salary from csr_levels.base_salary. Paid Units and Conversion % from csr_performance_monthly. Commission Tier auto-applied from commission_tiers based on conversion rate. Weekly Bonuses from weekly_bonus_log.
Fully automated. csr_promotion_thresholds drives promotion. csr_level_history records every level change. Payroll picks up current level automatically — HR cannot manually change CSR levels.
Crossing a tier threshold recalculates ALL units for that month at the higher rate. Backdating principle already specced in Day 1.
Clicking any commission cell opens slide-out showing raw csr_performance_monthly data — exact units ordered vs paid, weekly_bonus_log entries, tier calculation breakdown. Exportable as PDF for staff.

Step 1: HR reviews calculations. Step 2: HR clicks Request Sign-Off — status = Pending Director Approval. Step 3: Director reviews and clicks Approve Batch. Step 4: Accountant pays externally via bank transfer. Step 5: Accountant logs payment with payment_date, bank reference, payment_method in payroll table.
HR cannot mark payroll as paid. Director approves, Accountant executes.
HR-filtered view of staff activity. Not the same as system audit_log used by Directors — focused on employee behaviour patterns.
Employee name, date range, action type
Login times, logout times, actions taken (status changes, price edits, panel opens), volume per session
Customer PII beyond what the employee themselves accessed — audit log shows actions, not customer data
CSV export for disciplinary proceedings or management reviews

UI philosophy: utilitarian and batch-focused. Rapid logging of physical items coming in and going out. Strict guardrails prevent negative stock. Every action has a financial consequence tracked automatically.
Live Inventory (default), Goods Movement, Waybills, Returned and Trashed, Stock Adjustments
Live Inventory — real-time stock counts for all products
Warehouse Coordinator, Ops Manager, and Directors
Two tabs: Main Stock and Order Bump Stock. Same columns and logic, separate tables (inventory and order_bump_inventory).
Product Name, Variant/Colour, Waybill Stock, Reserved (Pending orders), Decremented (Cash Paid orders), Available to Sell (GENERATED ALWAYS AS — database calculated)
🟢 Green = healthy stock. 🟡 Yellow = below 20% of waybill stock. 🔴 Red = 10 or fewer units. Exact thresholds configurable.
CSR-set flag on the product. Visible here as an indicator. Does not affect the stock calculation — informational only.
Top right. Opens Goods Movement slide-out to dispatch stock to an agent.
Identical layout. Pulls from order_bump_inventory table.
Tracks the physical transfer of stock between warehouse and agents. Separate from Waybill Expenses which tracks the cost of movement.
Date, Product, Variant, Quantity, Movement Type (Sent/Returned/Waybill), From, To Agent, State, Logged By

Product dropdown, Variant dropdown, Quantity, Movement Type, Destination Agent, State. On save: formally transfers physical liability from HQ warehouse to agent. Accountant notified to log transport cost in Finance module.
When agent returns stock: Movement Type = Returned. from_agent_id populated. Stock re-enters available pool if condition is good.
All past movements searchable by product, agent, date range.
Tracks the cost of every inter-state dispatch. Related to Goods Movement but separate — Goods Movement = physical stock, Waybills = financial cost.
Date, Product, Batch, Quantity, Origin State, Destination Agent, State, Transport Cost, Courier, Payment Method, Status, Receipt URL
Company Paid, Agent to Pay and Deduct, Petty Cash. Selecting Agent to Pay and Deduct automatically posts a Waybill_Paid_By_Agent credit entry to the agent's ledger — no manual adjustment needed.
Google Drive link to transport invoice or receipt. Same pattern as Accountant expense receipts.
Product, Batch, Quantity, From State, To State, Agent, Courier, Transport Cost ₦, Payment Method, Receipt URL, Comments.
Warehouse (packed), In Transit, Signed Receipt (delivered and confirmed).
Date Logged, Product, Quantity, Returned By Agent, State, Condition, Reason/Comments
🔴 Trashed (unsellable — damaged, broken, soaked), 🟢 Redistributed (good condition — returned to agent stock or warehouse)
Agent, Product, Quantity, Condition (Trashed or Redistributed), Reason/Comments (mandatory).
AFTER INSERT on trashed_items WHERE condition = Trashed: subtracts quantity from waybill_stock in inventory table. Prevents ghost stock appearing as Available.
Good condition returns are re-entered into agent stock — no waybill_stock deduction. Logged for audit trail only.
Trashed items feed the Director's Shrinkage Report showing monthly NGN value of physical losses.
The most strictly controlled screen. Bridges the gap between physical warehouse count and digital stock count after theft, miscounts, or marketing samples.
Date, Product, Qty Adjusted, Adjustment Type, Comments, Logged By, Status/Approval
Product, Variant, Quantity Adjusted (positive or negative integer), Adjustment Type, Comments (mandatory).
Found/Surplus and Marketing_Sample adjustments where available stock stays above zero — approved automatically.

If adjustment pushes Available to Sell below zero: system blocks transaction. Red warning shown. Transaction enters Pending state. Director must log into Approvals panel and click Approve before trigger fires.
Coordinator sees Pending Director Approval on their adjustment row. Cannot proceed until resolved.
Includes 130+ Initial_Count_Correction entries imported from historical negative waybill entries during migration. Visible as archived records.
Shrinkage/Missing adjustments feed Director's monthly shrinkage cost report automatically.

UI philosophy: high-density, forensic layout. Chronological timelines, historical data, exception highlighting. The Auditor is the internal detective — they look at the ashes to see if someone started the fire deliberately. Most critical rule: absolute read-only access enforced at RBAC level, not just UI level. The Auditor cannot change a single status, delete a comment, or edit a price.
Audit Dashboard (default), Global Activity Log, Communication Forensics, Spot-Check Queues
Audit Dashboard — macro red flags and automated exception detection
Absolute read-only. Enforced at RBAC level — not UI-only. Every route returns data only. No write endpoints available to Auditor role.
The only action available. Routes through order_escalations table with reason code Auditor_Flag. Appears in Director Approvals panel.
Auto-filters the entire database to find unnatural behaviour patterns. Three alert widgets at top. Triage queue below.
Orders where status changed 4 or more times within the same calendar day. Indicates possible manipulation, customer confusion, or CSR gaming metrics.

Cash Paid orders with logistics_fee = 0 or null that were NOT approved by Ops Manager (no entry in order_escalations with Zero_Fee_Approval_Request resolved). These slipped through without authorisation.
Orders that moved from Pending to a terminal status (Failed, Cancelled) in under 3 minutes. Physically impossible to have a legitimate conversation and log a result in under 3 minutes.
Order ID, CSR Name, Time Assigned (first_contact_at), Time Failed (status change timestamp from order_status_history), Delta (seconds/minutes), Failure Reason Code. Sorted by shortest delta first.
Auditor opens order, adds note explaining suspicion, clicks Flag for Director. Creates order_escalations row with reason_code = Auditor_Flag. Director sees it in their Approvals panel.
Direct UI wrapper for the audit_log table. Every keystroke and dropdown change across the entire company. Uneditable by any user including Directors.
Timestamp, User + Role, Action Type, Record ID, Field Changed, Old Value, New Value
By Order ID, user name, or field name
By action type (edit_order, status_change, price_change, login, adjustment, deletion)
Filtering Field Changed = Price instantly surfaces all price modifications across all orders — primary fraud detection tool
CSV export for disciplinary proceedings or Director review
No row in audit_log can be edited or deleted by any user. PostgreSQL-level constraint.
V1 shows WATI export history and Pabbly reconciliation logs. V2 adds live WhatsApp chat mirror via WATI API.

Shows every export to Cash Paid Orders and Complaints/Refunds sheets. Columns: export_type, export_date, records_exported, status (Success/Failed), error_message. Auditor can verify that sequences fired correctly for paid orders.
Daily CRM order count vs Pabbly webhook count. Discrepancies flagged. Auditor investigates gaps — orders that came through Pabbly but did not appear in CRM or vice versa.

Live chronological read-only mirror of actual WhatsApp chat between company and customer phone number, pulled via WATI API. Auditor can verify CSR actually contacted customer, read exact messages sent and received. Deferred to V2 — V1 uses two Google Sheets, no direct API.
Price manipulation detection: Auditor sees price changed from ₦39,500 to ₦30,000 in Activity Log, then checks WATI mirror to see what was actually communicated to the customer.
Mathematically randomised daily compliance testing. Auditors cannot review 11,000 orders monthly — random sampling ensures coverage without bias.
Pulls 50 random orders targeting vulnerable statuses: Cash Paid, Failed, Cancelled, CSR Skipped, and recent stock adjustments. New sample generated each day.
Order ID, Audit Category, CSR/Agent, three action buttons
Cash_Paid_Verify, Rejected_Verify, CSR_Skipped, Shrinkage_Log, High_Bounce_Order, Rapid_Fail — from auditor_check_categories table
Pass ✅ (order is compliant), Flag ❌ (routes to Director via order_escalations with Auditor_Flag reason), Add Note 📝 (adds auditor_notes to auditor_spot_checks row)
X / 50 Audits Completed Today — visible at bottom of queue
View Audit Stats button shows historical pass/fail rates by CSR, agent, category, and period
Spot checks require Auditor to call customer directly for delivery verification — CRM cannot verify this but the Flag mechanism captures the outcome
Clicking any Order ID across any Auditor screen
Full order details — read-only. All fields visible but no edit capability. No Save button.
Complete status change timeline from order_status_history. Every status, who changed it, exact timestamp.
Full forensic log from audit_log for this specific order — every field change, old value, new value, user, timestamp. Same data as Global Activity Log filtered to this order.
Right side — V2 WATI Mirror tab
Read-only chronological WhatsApp chat log pulled via WATI API. V2 only.
Only action available. Mandatory note field. Creates escalation record.
26. Social Media Manager Interface — UI/UX Specification

UI philosophy: aggregated data visualisation only. Mirrors the Director dashboard analytical power but strips away all financial unit economics (CPO/CPS) and all PII. The SMM does not need a customer's phone number or home address to know if a TikTok ad is converting. Absolute rule: dashboard-only, read-only access. No order grids, no individual rows, zero PII. Enforced at RBAC level.
Performance Insights (default), Geographic Heatmaps, Product Winners
Performance Insights — the macro advertising funnel
Dashboard-only read-only. No order grids. No PII. RBAC-enforced.
CPO, CPS, individual order details, customer names, phone numbers, addresses, employee names, financial totals — all hidden from SMM.
Today, This Week, This Month, Custom
Facebook, TikTok, Jamisan Form, All — from order_sources table
Server re-fetches all charts on filter change. Same global filter pattern as Director dashboard.
Answers the ultimate media buying question: are these ads bringing serious buyers or window shoppers?

Waterfall chart: Raw Leads Generated → Validated (Interested to Scheduled) → Abandoned/Rejected → Cash Paid Sales → Final Conversion Rate. Source filter makes this funnel specific to Facebook, TikTok, or all sources.
Three segments: Female, Male, Undefined. Shows Leads, Sales, and Conversion Rate per segment. Undefined always shown — hiding it skews the demographic picture.
Conversion Rate by Day of Week
Horizontal bar chart. Tells SMM optimal days to publish content and run ads. Highest-converting days get more budget allocation.

SMM can see that a female-targeted TikTok creative has 70% female leads but only 12% conversion, while 30% male leads convert at 45%. Kills the creative and pivots targeting — without seeing any individual customer data.
State-by-state targeting matrix. Tells SMM exactly which states to include or exclude in Facebook and TikTok Ad Manager.
State, Raw Leads Generated, Cash Paid Sales, Final Conversion Rate (bar visualisation), Recommendation
Based on department_targets thresholds — not static labels.
Conversion rate above target_conversion_rate for the department. Increase ad spend here.
Conversion rate at or near base_conversion_rate. Healthy — keep current budget.
Conversion rate below base_conversion_rate but above 15%. Watch for decline.
Conversion rate below 15%. Add to Facebook/TikTok exclusion list immediately. Budget wasted here.

SMM sees Ogun state generating cheap clicks but converting at 9%. Adds Ogun to exclusion list, reroutes budget to Lagos (42%). CRM ties marketing metrics to actual cash collected — not just form submissions.
Ranked by conversion velocity. Tells SMM which products deserve the most creative energy and budget.
Rank, Product Name, Total Leads, Cash Paid, Conversion Rate, Trend vs Previous Period
Compares conversion rate for current period vs previous period of same length. Computed at query time.
📈 Upward trend with percentage gain. 📉 Downward trend with percentage loss and Review Creative warning if decline is significant. ➖ Stable.
Sorted by conversion rate descending. Product with highest Cash Paid ÷ Total Leads ratio ranks first.
Steep downward trend on a product that has been running the same creative for weeks = ad fatigue signal. SMM pauses campaign and refreshes creative.
Revenue amounts hidden. SMM sees conversion rates and lead volumes only — no ₦ figures.
Top right on all screens. Exports aggregated chart data as CSV.
The aggregated numbers visible on screen — conversion rates, lead counts, state performance. No individual order data, no customer details.
SMM exports to build targeting audiences in Facebook Ads Manager or present performance to Director.

UI philosophy: unrestricted read-only exploration. The Analyst can ask infinite multi-variable questions across the entire database without writing SQL and without risking live operational data. Transforms the CRM from a workflow tool into a predictive intelligence engine.
Deep Query Engine (default), Lead Decay and Cohorts, Fulfillment Forensics
Director P&L, SMM Insights, Ops Command Center, HR Punch-Clock. Same screens, same data, read-only access.
Read-only across entire database. No write endpoints. Aggregated CSV export permitted — no raw PII rows.
Aggregated data export only. Grouped summary metrics visible on screen. Individual order rows with customer names and phone numbers are Director-only export.
Standard dashboards answer pre-defined questions. The Deep Query Engine lets the Analyst ask any multi-variable question across the entire order database without writing raw SQL.

Same Airtable-style multi-condition filter already built for CSR interface — Match ALL / ANY conditions, Where / And / Or rows, field + operator + value + delete. Add Condition and Add Condition Group (OR logic). No new UI infrastructure.
All order fields: Department, Product, State, LGA, Agent, CSR, Status, DFO (Days From Order), Date Range, Sex, Price, Source, Order Bump, Failure Reason.
is exactly, is not, contains, does not contain, is greater than, is less than, is empty, is not empty.
Aggregated view showing: total matching records, total value at risk (₦ sum), average DFO in current status. Then paginated grid of matching records — read-only, no inline editing.
Analyst can group results by Agent, CSR, State, Product, or Status to surface patterns.
Save and name frequently used queries for quick reload.
Dropdown of previously saved queries — team shared, not personal only.
Aggregated CSV of the grouped summary — not raw individual rows with PII.
Analyst builds: Department = Beauty, Status = Pending, DFO > 3. Returns 142 records. Groups by Agent. Instantly reveals 90% of stale orders belong to one agent holding ₦2.8M in pipeline cash.
Mathematically proves how fast a lead decays. Provides hard data to defend or challenge operational rules like the 72-hour auto-abandon threshold.

DFO (Days From Order) on x-axis vs Conversion Rate on y-axis. Shows volume reaching each day stage and final conversion rate. Drop-off velocity column shows rate of decay per day. Flags Day 3+ as ⚠️ and Day 4+ as 🔴 Dead Zone.
orders.ordered_at vs orders.date_paid. DFO computed at query time. Groups all Cash Paid orders by how many days elapsed before payment.

Analyst can prove that extending auto-abandon from 72 hours to 5 days would result in dispatching riders at an 8% success rate — mathematically losing money on logistics costs. Director uses this to reject the change request with hard data.
Groups customers by the month of their first Cash Paid order. Tracks how many returned for a 2nd and 3rd purchase. Calculates 6-month Lifetime Value (LTV) per cohort.
SUM of all Cash Paid order values for a customer across their lifetime. Grouped by first-order cohort month.
Date Range, Product, Department. Source filter shows decay curve per ad channel.
Cross-references product types against geographical logistics. Answers: are certain products too difficult to deliver to certain places?
Product Name, State, Assigned (total orders), Delivered (Cash Paid), Success %, Primary Failure Reason (most common failure_reason_id for non-converted orders in this product-state combination)
🟢 50%+ success. 🟡 25-49%. 🔴 Below 25% — flagged for exclusion consideration.
State (default), Agent, LGA, CSR. Analyst switches the grouping variable to isolate different bottleneck types.
Auto-calculated from failure_reason_codes — most frequent failure reason for orders in this product-state cell. Surfaces patterns like Location Requires Waybill or Customer Switched Off.
Date Range, Department, Product — any combination.
Aggregated CSV — product-state summary only, no customer details.

Analyst spots 2-in-1 Speaker in Enugu converting at 12% with Primary Failure Reason = Location Requires Waybill. Speaker's weight makes last-mile delivery too expensive in remote Enugu LGAs. SMM excludes Enugu from all Gadget ad sets — company margin restored.
Jamisan ERP Spec — Day 1 Complete | Day 2: Database Schema on dbdiagram.io
